reviews.review_msg: Very dense material on which Holly's lectures do not really improve understanding. Holly is very strict and nitpicky when it comes to grading
She cannot teach. watch youtube if you want any chance at passing the class. Shes also rude when you ask her questions.
Grades really slow. Passive aggressive

Holly felt no sympathy during a difficult time, refusing to give an exam extension. As a disabled student trying to attend law school, this was extremely difficult. An otherwise straight-A semester was ruined. I had to jump through many extra hoops to make my dream happen. Someone should refuse her a small kindness that costs her 100x more pain.
Grading is arbitrary. You won't learn much from the course at all
Absolutely the worst class I have ever taken in my life. This class was bane of my existence. Dont ever wanna have a single philosophical thought because of this course.

Here's a burn-style student review you could drop anywhere:


---

This professor could make rocket launches sound boring. Lectures are incoherent rambles, questions get shot down with sarcasm, and grading feels like rolling dice. Philosophy of science should challenge you to think—here it just drains your will to care. Avoid at all costs.
Useless readings. Grading very subjective
Single handedly ruined my whole term. Very strict grader. Expects you to focus on her class to the detriment of other classes. Zero compassion and understanding for students

Professor Andersen is really disappointing. Her lectures are confusing, and it feels like she doesn't take the time to explain things clearly. When I asked for help, she seemed impatient and didn't give useful feedback on assignments. It's been a struggle to stay engaged in her class. Wouldn't recommend.

The readings are really obscure and difficult to parse. Had to spend several hours trying to make sense of obscure papers in this class. My grades in other classes suffered as a result. Holly is not helpful at all
Grading is very arbitrary, based on subjective opinion. Holly's justification for the grades she hands out does not make much sense
Tries to get help during office hours but Holly just asked me to read more stuff on my own. She does not care about helping students at all.
The grading felt pretty subjective. I don't think she actually read my entire assignment. The feedback seemed unrelated to what I wrote.

So many readings and the lectures are pretty useless. The professor is very demanding, goes on so many tangents and wants to cover a lot of material within the course. The pace of the course is too fast, making it difficult to keep up.

Holly is passionate to a fault. Expects students to spend too much time on her classes. We have students struggling with cost of living and then there are professors like Holly who expect that students cam devote all waking hours to academics. Some of us need to work through college. Such an elitist snob.

I would 100% take another class with Holly. She made a hard course so enjoyable. Even tho science is not my strong suit by any means, I thoroughly enjoyed this class. She made two hour lectures go by so quickly! Holly is also a very fair marker! This class had 2 papers and weekly in-class writings that were not hard at all.

Holly is a good professor, but she is almost passionate to a fault. She tends to go on tangents in lectures that can be useful if you really understand the course content, but if Philosophy of Science is not your thing, it can further complicate matters. Also, the readings are difficult so be prepared for that. However, Holly gives great feedback.
Holly is great at explaining complex concepts. She also provides detailed feedbacks in her office hours.

Dr. Andersen is really really great. Her passion is infectious and she clearly cares a lot about not only sharing the course content with her students but also about ensuring that everyone is following along. Phil of sci isn't really my fav area of phil so I was at times a bit lost but that wasn't her fault and really tries to keep people engaged.

Be prepared to spend more than 20 or 30 hrs every week juggling through precis and discussion questions on top of dense historical material. You often get the grade and feedback for the precis you submit one month later, but no need to worry! Holly will graciously offer you the opportunity to write another precis as a substitute!

Holly is an engaging lecturer and has a fair course structure. She also has weekly in-class writings that will keep your understanding in check, so make sure to show up and keep up with readings. Readings are difficult, but she explains the material well. Would definitely take her classes again!

Super passionate about what she teaches. Even if youre not personally interested, her passion is infectious. Really cares that her students understand material and assignments. Quick to respond to emails and open to feedback about how the course content could be adjusted for future students. Great prof.

Fall19 in-person. Holly is an excellent lecturer! Top quality prof at SFU. Philosophy readings are hard to understand but shes able to explain concepts that would otherwise be hard to explain in words. You could tell that she is intelligent and able to communicate her thoughts very well. I really learned a lot attending her class. Loved the topic.

Holly is passionate about this subject to a fault, she frequently assigns way too much assignment and work, and expects too much. She has very poor time management, trying to cram a 2h lecture into 1h. She has incredibly high expectations, as if this is a Masters level. She isn't a bad person, just deeply unaware of how overwhelming her class is.

Prof Andersen is such a great professor, I had to take PHIL341 as 'Science of Philosophy', and it was honestly one of the hardest Philosophy courses I have taken (lots of content), but the prof made it much more interesting. She's very enthusiastic on the subject and is nice. Definitely recommed her as a prof if you get the opportunity.

Holly is an amazing prof, super sweet and understanding. THIS CLASS IS NOT AN EASY BOOSTER. Definitely a lot of work involved, papers, final, and the course material as well can be difficult to grasp at times. The course overall is interesting and a good grade can be achieved through constantly studying and reviewing material.

Holly is super kind and helpful. This class has a lot of reading and some parts may be hard to understand without previous knowledge of physics. If you are looking for an easy credit, this class is not the one! Around 75% of the time, I had no clue what was going on, but with a solid amount of effort, I actually managed to do pretty well.

Absolutely loved Holly. Never have I seen a prof so quick to respond  to complicated questions with great answers. Metaphysics is hard, but she has a knack for explanation. Readings are insane, but she goes through them in class. Take TONS of notes, you'll need them for the final. I hope to get her again.

An amazing professor! She is incredibly knowledgeable and passionate, makes the material interesting and makes sure everyone is on board before moving on. It's really necessary to attend lectures because the readings are quite difficult. But she makes everything clear. Short in-class writing assignment every week.

Holly is an amazing professor! She is incredibly knowledgable about the subject matter and puts together a great reading list. She really encourages student discussion and gives great feedback. If she has a reputation for being tough it is only because she really wants her students to do well and pushes them to do well.

Probably the most boring class I've taken in my 3 years. She's a enthusiastic prof and is pretty nice, however the material is really boring which made it difficult to remain focused in class. Lectures were recorded, however its always best to go because her train of thought and organization of the lecture is chaotic.

No midterms but god the material is so boring. She takes a long time talking in lectures and throws in a bunch of additional details that you dont need to know. If youre trying to boost your grade I wouldnt recomend this cause the information is trash and not interesting at all.

Holly was an amazing professor! I'm not even a philosophy major, but I would most definitely take a class with her again. She genuinely cares about her students and wants you to do well. Lectures are also somewhat humorous. Great prof!

Judging by her early reviews, it seems as though she wasn't especially good in 2011. If this is the case, it is safe to say that she has improved tremendously. Her lectures are interesting, and her examples are both memorable and entertaining. I've had her twice, and I'm sad that I won't be able to have her again; she's the best prof. I've had.

I never used my textbook. she summarizes everything for you and explains the concepts quite well. Go to the lectures! Notes are extremely helpful for assignments and final. No midterms and she gives you questions that are going to be on the final exam.   Assignments are not that hard. You are given clear instructions.

She does very well in engaging the class! I've never found science interesting, plus I've never taken a philosophy course so the two combined made me reluctant.  However she's very charismatic and knowledgable.  This course has actually made me interested in philosophy and the sciences, would highly recommend it.
You have to buy the text, she uses it alot. Really interesting extra examples to go along with the text. Weekly writing.
Interesting course, decent prof.

Holly is one of the best professors of Philosphy at SFU. She is incredibly precise and clear with her explanations and uses the relevant texts very well (you never feel like you've wasted money). Downside: She's a super tough marker. Be prepared to receive lower paper marks than you are used to. Also: Participate! She loves students who speak up.

Dr. Andersen is okay. Her lectures are quite helpful in understanding the content, but she uses very pretentious and unnecessary language in explaining the content which is a bit unhelpful. Her tests are very fair though. Overall, not the worst prof.

She is the best professor I've ever had. Her lectures are interesting and very clear. She explains the readings so well and with such ease that you will completely understand them once you go back and read them again with her explanation. Midterm and final were fair. Best. Prof. Ever.

Everyone seems upset by the final, but the reason it was made so hard was because the department has guidelines regarding what students should be getting, so she had to balance it with the easy midterm.  ANYWAY.  I think she was a great prof.  Some of the readings were challenging, but being a philosophy course, one should expect to be difficulty.

She gave decent explanations of the material and posted all lecture slides online which was very helpful for studying for the final. As long as you actually do the readings for this class (which many people didn't) it is not too difficult.

I don't have a problem of calling her "doctor", people work hard for a PhD, I can understand that. However, she is definitely not as approachable as most profs and can really get in your face unpleasantly. Had more luck on papers than doing the final, TA was a lot more generous and helpful even if tutorials were more or less a recap of lecture.

Doesn't help much in class since her answers to every question is irrelevant. Why would a prof go out of the way to make the average 40% AND THEN curve it. Also, she won't respond to everything BUT doctor andersen since she has a phD in philosophy (also a mistake) I'll call her a doctor when she can medically aid in someone's health.

The final was closed book reading comprehension that was never even talked about in class. Papers marked hard. If you need to lower the average, make the course harder or add a paper; don't just make the final ridiculous..I bet TA's would of failed...ruined Philosophy for me and I worked really hard for a C+

She definitely doesn't know how to teach.Average was 39% for this semester and C average for papers,What do you expect to end up with? Bingoo!! C grade.Avoid this prof even if you have to change your school.she basically wants everyone to fail or get C Grade.She has something for Doctoral degree as she asks people to call her with her Dr thingie.
Midterm was not hard and average was high. However, pro was unhappy with that results and aimed to make the final average around 40%. That's why I ended up just with B- .....(
DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH THIS PROF. SHE IS ABSOLUTELY TERRIBLE. DOES NOT EXPLAIN THINGS WELL AND WANTS STUDENTS TO DO POORLY. FINAL EXAM AVG WAS 40%.

She was extremely unapproachable, and her lectures were difficult to follow. She records all lectures and posts the slides online, which was helpful for the papers. Overall, I would not recommend this course to anyone since most people in my tutorials also found the material really hard to understand.

dont recommand her class because she grades hardly. She aims an avg of 45% for the final exam which is 25% of the overall grade. 50% overall for two papers and class avg for the papers were around C range. Midterm was simple. If you planning to take her course, be prepared.
hard for esl, not good at explaining at all. do not take this class unless it is required
One of the worst profs I've ever had at sfu. If you take her you will regret it.
Lectures are very well put together and the tutorials really compliment the course work. Highly recommend this class to any arts students looking for a not so science heavy science credit.
Very good at explaining concepts. Engaging lectures.

To me, this course was really interesting towards the beginning but the later reading were either to dense or boring. The papers were a great exercise for concise writing but she is the only prof i have ever had that scales down! If you want a course that will both challenge and captivate you, I recommend this course.

Dry and scattered reading material: is this a philosophy class- or is this quantum physics/neuroscience 101?  Topics were pretentious and hard to read through. Lectures were mostly discussion based, but her explanations were clear and concise. More structure would have been nice, as opposed to 2hrs of keeners quoting wikipedia. Fair grading.

She is very helpful when it comes to understanding the material. She asks if anyone is clear or not after each point she makes. She will also take the time to answer parts that you don't understand after class time. Very good prof. I will definitely take her again! And she makes good cupcakes!

Amazing professor. Wonderful person, very very helpful. Will take her time to make sure everyone understands. Patient with students and gives helpful feedback. There is not a single thing I can find that is wrong with her. Only thing is, she distracts you sometimes in lecture because she's so good looking.

Definitely the best philosophy prof ever. She's crystal clear, and easy to understand - able to whip up helpful diagrams and analogies seemingly at whim. She facilitates engaging class discussions. Her lectures are never boring.
best prof ever
Awesome professor!! Very good at explaining, very very very nice person. Try to take as many courses with her as you can!
